summary.intent: Resolve three unresolved Codex review threads on PR #727 before pushing: add Dioxus hidden-input committed-state resync, add missing `.ars-sr-input` CSS to E2E fixtures, and add visible primitive checkbox styling to plain widget examples.
summary.outcome: All three review fixes were implemented and verified (focused wasm regression passing, clippy clean, all cargo checks green), then amend-committed locally as 'fix(checkbox): resolve review and coverage feedback' — no push made per user instruction.

repo: GraphQL reviewThreads returns author login as `chatgpt-codex-connector`, not `codex` — filters using `contains("codex")` with `ascii_downcase` are required to match Codex review threads.

Dioxus RSX formatter (`dx fmt`) mangles raw multi-line CSS inside `style {}` nodes, stripping selector lines and breaking the output — CSS must be stored in a module-level Rust `const` and interpolated as `"{CONSTANT}"` inside the style node.
Leptos formatter (`leptosfmt`) reindents inline CSS and introduces trailing whitespace that fails `git diff --check` — same CSS-constant pattern is required for Leptos plain examples.
`MountedData::downcast` in Dioxus returns a borrowed element and cannot be returned through `and_then` across a closure boundary — the downcast must be consumed within the mounted-handle scope.

The Dioxus hidden-input resync via `MountedData` downcast alone is insufficient after label activation; a DOM query by the core-owned hidden input id is needed to force the property back to the committed state.
`ars-base.css` defines the canonical `.ars-sr-input` visually-hidden rule — E2E fixture stylesheets must mirror this exactly rather than introducing a near-copy with different values.

code.finding: Hidden input mounted handle storage and committed-state resync after native change events added here; final approach queries hidden input by core-owned id rather than using MountedData downcast.
New browser regression `checkbox_controlled_hidden_input_tracks_committed_state_after_label_click` added, mirroring the Leptos regression pattern from lines 240-335 of the Leptos wasm test.
Plain checkbox primitive styling stored in module-level `PLAIN_CHECKBOX_STYLES` const and interpolated in RSX `style` node to survive `dx fmt` rewrites.
Same CSS-constant pattern applied to Leptos plain example to avoid leptosfmt trailing-whitespace issues.
Missing `.ars-sr-input` visually-hidden rule added, aligned with ars-base.css canonical definition.
Same `.ars-sr-input` rule added to Dioxus fixture stylesheet.

workflow: When a review thread filter misses threads, verify the exact author login field from GraphQL rather than assuming a short alias — author login shapes differ between REST and GraphQL and between bot accounts.

Dioxus wasm tests require `WASM_BINDGEN_TEST_ONLY_WEB=1` env var and `--features web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown` flags; test runner approval can time out on first attempt and should be retried once.
Run `cargo xfmt` before each `cargo check` or wasm test cycle to avoid formatter-introduced regressions invalidating check results.
`git diff --check` should be run as the final gate before committing to catch formatter-introduced trailing whitespace.
Amending a local unpushed commit (not a push) is safe for bundling a Codecov config change with review fixes in a single logical commit.

friction: Initial Codex thread filter used `author.login == 'codex'` which silently missed all threads authored by `chatgpt-codex-connector`, requiring manual identification via linked comment URL.
MountedData downcast approach for Dioxus hidden-input resync compiled but failed the browser regression at runtime — required a second implementation switch to DOM id-based query.
Lifetime error: `MountedData::downcast` borrow cannot escape the mounted-handle closure, causing a compile failure that required refactoring the helper.
Dioxus RSX formatter destroyed inline multi-line CSS on the first `cargo xfmt` run, requiring multiple patch/inspect/reformat cycles to stabilize the style block approach before switching to a const.
Patch context mismatches after formatter rewrites made `apply_patch` fail repeatedly on the Dioxus input file, requiring raw `sed -n ... l` output to reconstruct exact file content.
Wasm test command approval timed out once, requiring a retry.
open_items: Changes are committed locally but not pushed — review threads on PR #727 are not yet replied to or resolved on GitHub.
The three review thread replies/resolutions on GitHub still need to be posted once the push is authorized.
The plain primitive checkbox examples now have scoped local CSS; if a shared example stylesheet is added later, these constants should be migrated there.
